assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable is a recognised monetary item between group entities with different functional currencies.
The related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup foreign-currency receivable is relevant to assess under the fair value hedge model because IFRS 9 permits the foreign currency risk of certain intragroup monetary items to be designated in consolidated financial statements.
